Full job description
Leadership Development Manager / L&D Manager | Children’s Services | Health & Social Care
Home-Based (UK) | Full-Time, Permanent | £45,000 – £50,000 + Car Allowance

I’m working with a national health and social care provider to recruit a Leadership Development Manager supporting operational leaders across Children’s Services.

This role focuses on developing Registered Managers and Regional Managers, with a strong emphasis on induction, leadership capability and retention.

You’ll be working closely with HR, Operations and Learning & Development teams, helping to improve how managers are supported in role, particularly during their first 6–12 months.

This is a practical, hands-on role. It’s not just about designing training, it’s about working directly with managers and understanding what’s happening on the ground.

What You’ll Be Doing

Supporting the onboarding and induction of new Registered Managers
Delivering and embedding leadership development programmes
Coaching managers in role, particularly during their first year
Working alongside Regional Managers to support performance and consistency
Using feedback and data to identify risks and areas for improvement• Contributing to internal talent development and succession planning
Key Responsibilities

Develop and deliver leadership development activity across services
Work with HR, L&D, Operations and Quality teams to align priorities
Track progress of managers and highlight any risks early
Support consistent and structured induction and development pathways
Build strong relationships with senior stakeholders across the organisation
Use insight, feedback and performance data to improve outcomes
Ensure learning activity is captured and tracked appropriately
What You’ll Get

£45,000 – £50,000 salary (depending on experience)
Car allowance
Home-based role with UK travel
Opportunity to shape leadership development in a growing organisation
What You’ll Need to Succeed

Background in health and social care (Children’s Services, residential care, supported living, mental health or similar)• Good understanding of the Registered Manager role
Experience in leadership development, L&D, training or operational leadership
Confident working with and influencing managers at different levels
Able to work independently and manage your own workload
A valid driving licence and access to your own vehicle
Travel Requirements

Travel – This role requires regular travel across the UK to support services. You will need a valid driving licence and access to your own vehicle.

About Tide

Tide Partnership is acting as a Recruitment Consultancy for this vacancy. Tide Partnership Ltd is registered in England & Wales and is an Equal Opportunities Employer. Please refer to our Privacy Policy for details on how we process your data.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: £45,000.00-£50,000.00 per year

personalisedIntro: I’ve spent my career stepping into roles where performance, consistency and day-to-day leadership all need tightening at the same time. This role fits the way I work: close to the operational reality, focused on what managers need in their first year, and comfortable using data, feedback and direct support to improve outcomes.
whyThisRole: The work itself is a strong match for how I’ve led before: helping managers get established, improving how teams are supported, and using evidence to spot risks early. I’m at my best when I can combine coaching, practical problem-solving and operational follow-through rather than treating development as a classroom exercise.

The chance to shape that support in a growing organisation is the part that stands out to me.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role really needs someone who can build trust with managers quickly, understand what happens after induction, and stay close enough to the detail to spot problems early. It also needs someone who can work across HR, Operations, L&D and Quality without losing sight of the practical support managers need on the ground.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical manager support
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on what helps a new manager settle, make sound decisions and avoid avoidable mistakes in the first 6–12 months.

focusAreasToBring.title: Early risk visibility
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would use feedback, reporting and direct contact with managers to spot issues early and act before they become bigger problems.

focusAreasToBring.title: Consistency across services
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would look for simple ways to make induction, expectations and support more consistent without losing sight of local context.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background is in operational leadership where performance, people and service quality all have to hold together at once. I’ve worked in healthcare, regulated environments and multi-site settings, so I’m comfortable with the mix of coaching, structure and follow-through this role calls for.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first 6-12 months getting close to the manager journey, understanding where induction breaks down, and helping make support more consistent. I’d also expect to spend time with the data and with stakeholders so I can help spot risks earlier and make the development offer more practical for the people using it.
companyHighlights: Tide Partnership is recruiting for a national health and social care provider.
The role is home-based with regular UK travel.
The brief says there is scope to shape leadership development in a growing organisation.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in practical, hands-on environments where I can build relationships, stay close to the detail and work independently. The mix of home-based working, travel and collaboration across functions suits the way I’ve operated in previous roles.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would spend time learning how Registered Managers and Regional Managers are currently inducted, supported and tracked, and where the main pressure points sit.
I would work with HR, Operations, L&D and Quality to understand current activity, review available feedback and performance data, and identify the most obvious risks or gaps.
I would start tightening the support offer around induction, coaching and progress tracking so managers get clearer, more consistent help in role.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, practical approach to helping managers settle, perform and stay supported in role.
closingProofPoints: I improved Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51 in NHS primary care.
I’ve led cross-site improvement activity using operational data and frontline insight.
I’ve managed operations through growth while keeping delivery and resilience in focus.
